# Landing Page + Docs Site Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Ship `frp-operator.vercel.app` — combined landing page + Astro Starlight docs site with auto-generated reference layer (CRDs, Helm values, operator flags) deployed via Vercel.

**Architecture:** Astro 5 + Starlight in `site/` (separate pnpm tree, no Go-module pollution). Landing on `/` is a custom `pages/index.astro` that bypasses Starlight; docs on `/docs/*` use Starlight. Reference docs generated by `crd-ref-docs` (CRDs), `helm-docs` (values), and a new `hack/gen-flags-doc.go` (operator flags); generated MDX is committed to git so Vercel needs no Go toolchain. CI drift gate keeps generated content in sync with code. Tokens (cream + amber-600 + Fredoka/Nunito) live in `site/src/styles/tokens.css` and are consumed by both landing and Starlight.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 5, @astrojs/starlight, @astrojs/tailwind, Tailwind 3, pnpm 9, Node 20, crd-ref-docs (elastic), helm-docs (norwoodj), Go AST.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-landing-docs-design.md`.

import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import starlight from '@astrojs/starlight';

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://frp-operator.vercel.app',
  integrations: [
    tailwind({ applyBaseStyles: false }),
    starlight({

      customCss: [

        './src/styles/starlight-overrides.css',

        { label: 'Reference',       autogenerate: { directory: 'reference' } },

});

## Phase 2 — auto-generation pipeline

### Task 5: Tool installs in Makefile

**Files:** Modify: `Makefile`

- [ ] **Step 1: Find the existing `controller-gen` install pattern**

```bash
grep -n "controller-gen" Makefile | head -10
```

Note: the repo uses a versioned-binary install pattern under `bin/`. New tools follow the same shape.

- [ ] **Step 2: Append tool versions + install rules to `Makefile`**

Locate the section that defines `CONTROLLER_GEN_VERSION` (or equivalent) and add alongside it:

```make
CRD_REF_DOCS_VERSION ?= v0.1.0
HELM_DOCS_VERSION    ?= v1.14.2

CRD_REF_DOCS = $(LOCALBIN)/crd-ref-docs-$(CRD_REF_DOCS_VERSION)
HELM_DOCS    = $(LOCALBIN)/helm-docs-$(HELM_DOCS_VERSION)

.PHONY: crd-ref-docs
crd-ref-docs: $(CRD_REF_DOCS)
$(CRD_REF_DOCS): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(CRD_REF_DOCS),github.com/elastic/crd-ref-docs,$(CRD_REF_DOCS_VERSION))

.PHONY: helm-docs
helm-docs: $(HELM_DOCS)
$(HELM_DOCS): $(LOCALBIN)
	$(call go-install-tool,$(HELM_DOCS),github.com/norwoodj/helm-docs/cmd/helm-docs,$(HELM_DOCS_VERSION))
```

If `go-install-tool` isn't already defined in the Makefile, mirror what's used for `controller-gen`. (The kubebuilder Makefile template defines it; this repo started from kubebuilder so it should already exist.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify install**

```bash
make crd-ref-docs helm-docs
ls bin/crd-ref-docs-* bin/helm-docs-*
```

Expected: both binaries present in `bin/`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add Makefile
git commit -m "feat(site): add crd-ref-docs + helm-docs tool installs"
```

### Task 6: crd-ref-docs templates

**Files:** Create: `hack/crd-ref-templates/markdown/*.tpl`

The default crd-ref-docs Markdown templates produce headings like `# CRDName`. We need Starlight frontmatter (`---\ntitle: ...\n---`) at the top of each emitted file. crd-ref-docs accepts a `--templates-dir` containing override templates.

- [ ] **Step 1: Get the default template set**

```bash
mkdir -p hack/crd-ref-templates
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/elastic/crd-ref-docs /tmp/crd-ref-docs-src
cp -r /tmp/crd-ref-docs-src/templates/markdown/* hack/crd-ref-templates/
rm -rf /tmp/crd-ref-docs-src
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Patch the top-level template to emit Starlight frontmatter**

Open `hack/crd-ref-templates/gv_list.tpl` (the file that renders the per-group page; verify with `ls hack/crd-ref-templates/`). Replace the very top with:

```gotmpl
---
title: {{ "API reference" }}
description: Generated CRD reference for frp-operator.
---

```

Then keep the rest of the original template body unchanged.

- [ ] **Step 3: Smoke-test render**

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/crd-ref-out
bin/crd-ref-docs-* \
  --source-path=./api/v1alpha1 \
  --source-path=./pkg/cloudprovider/digitalocean/v1alpha1 \
  --source-path=./pkg/cloudprovider/localdocker/v1alpha1 \
  --templates-dir=hack/crd-ref-templates \
  --renderer=markdown \
  --output-path=/tmp/crd-ref-out
head -5 /tmp/crd-ref-out/out.md
rm -rf /tmp/crd-ref-out
```

Expected: file starts with `---\ntitle: API reference\n...`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add hack/crd-ref-templates/
git commit -m "feat(site): crd-ref-docs Starlight templates"
```

### Task 7: helm-docs template

**Files:** Create: `hack/helm-values-template.gotmpl`

helm-docs is template-driven: each chart's `values.yaml` comments → table. We provide a Starlight-flavored MDX template.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write `hack/helm-values-template.gotmpl`**

```gotmpl
---
title: Helm values
description: Generated reference for the frp-operator chart's values.yaml.
---

# {{ template "chart.name" . }} values

{{ template "chart.description" . }}

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|-----|------|---------|-------------|
{{- range .Values }}
| `{{ .Key }}` | {{ .Type }} | `{{ .Default }}` | {{ .Description }} |
{{- end }}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Smoke-test render**

```bash
bin/helm-docs-* \
  --chart-search-root=charts/frp-operator \
  --template-files=../../hack/helm-values-template.gotmpl \
  --output-file=/tmp/helm-values-test.mdx
head -10 /tmp/helm-values-test.mdx
rm -f /tmp/helm-values-test.mdx
```

Expected: starts with frontmatter, contains a `| Key | Type ...` table.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add hack/helm-values-template.gotmpl
git commit -m "feat(site): helm-docs MDX template"
```

### Task 8: gen-flags-doc.go (TDD)

**Files:**
- Create: `hack/gen-flags-doc.go`
- Create: `hack/gen-flags-doc_test.go`
- Create: `hack/testdata/gen-flags/flags.go` (input fixture)
- Create: `hack/testdata/gen-flags/expected.mdx` (golden)

Generator parses `pkg/operator/flags.go`, finds the `LoadConfigFromArgs` body, walks `fs.{String,Bool,Int,Float64,Duration}Var` calls plus the env-var fallbacks in `applyEnv`, and emits an MDX table.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write input fixture `hack/testdata/gen-flags/flags.go`**

A minimal slice of `pkg/operator/flags.go` shape, just enough to test the parser:

```go
package operator

import "flag"

type Config struct {
	LeaderElection bool
	LogLevel       string
}

func LoadConfigFromArgs(args []string) (*Config, error) {
	cfg := &Config{}
	fs := flag.NewFlagSet("frp-operator", flag.ContinueOnError)
	fs.BoolVar(&cfg.LeaderElection, "leader-elect", false, "Enable leader election.")
	fs.StringVar(&cfg.LogLevel, "log-level", "info", "Log level (debug|info|warn|error).")
	if err := fs.Parse(args); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return cfg, nil
}

func applyEnv(cfg *Config) error {
	return nil
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write golden `hack/testdata/gen-flags/expected.mdx`**

```mdx
---
title: Operator flags
description: Generated reference for frp-operator binary flags and env vars.
---

# Operator flags

Generated from `pkg/operator/flags.go`. Do not edit by hand — run `make site-gen`.

| Flag | Type | Default | Env var | Description |
|------|------|---------|---------|-------------|
| `--leader-elect` | `bool` | `false` |  | Enable leader election. |
| `--log-level` | `string` | `"info"` |  | Log level (debug\|info\|warn\|error). |
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Write the failing test `hack/gen-flags-doc_test.go`**

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"testing"
)

func TestGenerate_Golden(t *testing.T) {
	want, err := os.ReadFile("testdata/gen-flags/expected.mdx")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("read expected: %v", err)
	}
	var got bytes.Buffer
	if err := generate("testdata/gen-flags/flags.go", &got); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("generate: %v", err)
	}
	if !bytes.Equal(bytes.TrimSpace(want), bytes.TrimSpace(got.Bytes())) {
		t.Fatalf("mismatch:\nwant:\n%s\n\ngot:\n%s", want, got.String())
	}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./hack/...
```

Expected: failure (no `generate` function yet).

- [ ] **Step 5: Implement `hack/gen-flags-doc.go`**

```go
//go:build ignore_main_for_test
// +build ignore_main_for_test

package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"go/ast"
	"go/parser"
	"go/token"
	"io"
	"os"
	"strings"
)

const header = `---
title: Operator flags
description: Generated reference for frp-operator binary flags and env vars.
---

# Operator flags

Generated from ` + "`pkg/operator/flags.go`" + `. Do not edit by hand — run ` + "`make site-gen`" + `.

| Flag | Type | Default | Env var | Description |
|------|------|---------|---------|-------------|
`

type flagDef struct {
	name, typ, def, env, desc string
}

func main() {
	src := "pkg/operator/flags.go"
	if len(os.Args) > 1 {
		src = os.Args[1]
	}
	if err := generate(src, os.Stdout); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
}

func generate(srcPath string, out io.Writer) error {
	fset := token.NewFileSet()
	f, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, srcPath, nil, parser.ParseComments)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", srcPath, err)
	}
	envMap := collectEnvMap(f)
	flags := collectFlags(f, envMap)
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	buf.WriteString(header)
	for _, fl := range flags {
		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "| `--%s` | `%s` | `%s` | %s | %s |\n",
			fl.name, fl.typ, fl.def, fl.env, escapePipe(fl.desc))
	}
	_, err = io.Copy(out, &buf)
	return err
}

// collectFlags walks LoadConfigFromArgs body for fs.<Type>Var(... ).
func collectFlags(f *ast.File, envMap map[string]string) []flagDef {
	var out []flagDef
	for _, decl := range f.Decls {
		fn, ok := decl.(*ast.FuncDecl)
		if !ok || fn.Name.Name != "LoadConfigFromArgs" || fn.Body == nil {
			continue
		}
		ast.Inspect(fn.Body, func(n ast.Node) bool {
			call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr)
			if !ok {
				return true
			}
			sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
			if !ok {
				return true
			}
			typ, ok := varTypeFromMethod(sel.Sel.Name)
			if !ok || len(call.Args) < 4 {
				return true
			}
			ptr, ok := call.Args[0].(*ast.UnaryExpr)
			if !ok {
				return true
			}
			fieldName := fieldNameOf(ptr)
			out = append(out, flagDef{
				name: litString(call.Args[1]),
				typ:  typ,
				def:  litRender(call.Args[2]),
				env:  formatEnv(envMap[fieldName]),
				desc: litString(call.Args[3]),
			})
			return true
		})
	}
	return out
}

// collectEnvMap walks applyEnv body to map field name → env-var name.
func collectEnvMap(f *ast.File) map[string]string {
	m := map[string]string{}
	for _, decl := range f.Decls {
		fn, ok := decl.(*ast.FuncDecl)
		if !ok || fn.Name.Name != "applyEnv" || fn.Body == nil {
			continue
		}
		ast.Inspect(fn.Body, func(n ast.Node) bool {
			as, ok := n.(*ast.AssignStmt)
			if !ok || len(as.Lhs) != 1 || len(as.Rhs) != 1 {
				return true
			}
			lhs, ok := as.Lhs[0].(*ast.SelectorExpr)
			if !ok {
				return true
			}
			env := findGetenvArg(as.Rhs[0])
			if env != "" {
				m[lhs.Sel.Name] = env
			}
			return true
		})
	}
	return m
}

func findGetenvArg(expr ast.Expr) string {
	var found string
	ast.Inspect(expr, func(n ast.Node) bool {
		call, ok := n.(*ast.CallExpr)
		if !ok {
			return true
		}
		sel, ok := call.Fun.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
		if !ok || sel.Sel.Name != "Getenv" {
			return true
		}
		if len(call.Args) > 0 {
			found = litString(call.Args[0])
		}
		return false
	})
	return found
}

func varTypeFromMethod(name string) (string, bool) {
	switch name {
	case "BoolVar":
		return "bool", true
	case "StringVar":
		return "string", true
	case "IntVar":
		return "int", true
	case "Float64Var":
		return "float64", true
	case "DurationVar":
		return "duration", true
	}
	return "", false
}

func fieldNameOf(u *ast.UnaryExpr) string {
	sel, ok := u.X.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
	if !ok {
		return ""
	}
	return sel.Sel.Name
}

func litString(e ast.Expr) string {
	bl, ok := e.(*ast.BasicLit)
	if !ok || bl.Kind != token.STRING {
		return ""
	}
	return strings.Trim(bl.Value, `"`)
}

func litRender(e ast.Expr) string {
	switch v := e.(type) {
	case *ast.BasicLit:
		return v.Value
	case *ast.Ident:
		return v.Name
	case *ast.SelectorExpr:
		return litRenderIdent(v)
	}
	return ""
}

func litRenderIdent(e ast.Expr) string {
	if sel, ok := e.(*ast.SelectorExpr); ok {
		return sel.Sel.Name
	}
	if id, ok := e.(*ast.Ident); ok {
		return id.Name
	}
	return ""
}

func formatEnv(s string) string {
	if s == "" {
		return ""
	}
	return "`" + s + "`"
}

func escapePipe(s string) string { return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "|", `\|`) }
```

(Note: build tag `//go:build ignore_main_for_test` is in place so `go test ./hack/...` doesn't compile `main()` into the test binary; the test imports the package directly via `package main`.)

```mdx
---
title: frp-operator docs
description: Karpenter-shaped operator that gives every Service a public IP via frps.
---

frp-operator provisions cheap cloud frps exits and bin-packs your tunnels onto them, so any Kubernetes Service can have a routable address regardless of inbound network constraints.

These docs follow [Diátaxis](https://diataxis.fr):

- **Getting started** — step-by-step tutorials for first install and first tunnel.
- **Guides** — recipe-style how-to pages, one task per page.
- **Concepts** — explanations of the model (CRDs, scheduling, disruption).
- **Reference** — auto-generated CRD fields, Helm values, and operator flags.

If you're new, start with [Install on kind](/docs/getting-started/install-kind).
```

### Task 11: Guides stubs (10 pages)

**Files:** Create: `site/src/content/docs/guides/*.mdx` (10 files)

Each file is a frontmatter + caution stub. Use the loop below in your shell rather than typing each one out — but the actual `frontmatter -> slug` mapping must match the spec exactly.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the 10 stub files**

Create with this content template (vary `title` + `description` + `slug`):

```bash
mkdir -p site/src/content/docs/guides
for entry in \
  "allow-ports|Restrict exposed ports|Use ExitPool allowPorts to limit which ports the operator hands out." \
  "pin-region|Pin tunnels to specific regions|Use ExitPool requirements to anchor exits to a topology.kubernetes.io/region." \
  "rotate-exits|Rotate exits on schedule|Combine drift expiration with disruption budgets to roll exits weekly." \
  "cap-monthly-traffic|Cap monthly traffic per pool|Use ExitPool limits to enforce frp.operator.io/monthlyTrafficGB ceilings." \
  "multi-pool-tiers|Multi-pool gold/silver tiers|Run two ExitPools with different ProviderClasses and label selectors." \
  "migrate-from-frps|Migrate from raw frps|Move existing frps configurations into ExitPool + Tunnel resources." \
  "upgrade-crds|Upgrade CRDs|Use the frp-operator-crd chart's full Helm lifecycle for schema bumps." \
  "prometheus-scrape|Set up Prometheus scrape|Enable metrics.service and metrics.serviceMonitor values for Prometheus Operator." \
  "metrics-networkpolicy|Use NetworkPolicy for metrics isolation|Default-deny ingress + allow only from prometheusNamespace." \
  "multi-replica-leader|Run multi-replica with leader election|Bump replicas + leaderElection.enabled for high availability." ; do
  IFS='|' read -r slug title desc <<< "$entry"
  cat > "site/src/content/docs/guides/${slug}.mdx" <<EOF
---
title: ${title}
description: ${desc}
---

:::caution
Stub page — full recipe lands in a follow-up.
:::
EOF
done
```

const items = [
  {
    title: "Many tunnels, one exit",
    body: "Bin-pack many Services onto a single frps process. Auto port arbitration, no overlap.",
    vs: "vs inlets-operator: 1 VM per Service",
    icon: `<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="3" y="9" width="18" height="11" rx="2"/><path d="M7 9V6a5 5 0 0 1 10 0v3"/></svg>`,
  },
  {
    title: "Provision and schedule",
    body: "The operator decides when to scale a pool, which exit a new tunnel binds to, and when to consolidate. Drift, emptiness, expiration — all built in.",
    vs: "vs chisel-operator: provisions, doesn't schedule",
    icon: `<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M12 2v4M12 18v4M4.93 4.93l2.83 2.83M16.24 16.24l2.83 2.83M2 12h4M18 12h4M4.93 19.07l2.83-2.83M16.24 7.76l2.83-2.83"/></svg>`,
  },
  {
    title: "Cheapest-fit + budget caps",
    body: "Picks the lowest-priced instance type that satisfies pool requirements. Pool-level Limits cap CPU, bandwidth, and monthly traffic before you wake up to a bill.",
    vs: "No surprise invoices",
    icon: `<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"/><path d="M12 6v6l4 2"/></svg>`,
  },
];
---
<section class="features">
  <div class="wrap">
    <div class="section-label">Features</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Tunnels that pay rent.</h2>
    <p class="section-sub">Other operators hand each Service its own VM. We bin-pack and we shop for the cheapest fit.</p>
    <div class="feature-grid">
      {items.map((it) => (
        <div class="feature">
          <div class="feature-icon" set:html={it.icon}></div>
          <h3>{it.title}</h3>
          <p>{it.body}</p>
          <span class="vs">{it.vs}</span>
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<style>
  .features { padding: 64px 0; }
  .feature-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 20px; }
  .feature { background: var(--bg-elev-1); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 28px; transition: transform 200ms, box-shadow 200ms; }
  .feature:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); }
  .feature-icon { width: 44px; height: 44px; background: var(--accent-lo); border-radius: 12px; display: grid; place-items: center; color: var(--accent); margin-bottom: 18px; }
  .feature-icon :global(svg) { width: 22px; height: 22px; stroke-width: 2; }
  .feature h3 { font-family: var(--font-heading); font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .feature p { color: var(--ink-muted); font-size: 15px; }
  .feature .vs { display: inline-block; margin-top: 12px; font-size: 12px; color: var(--ink-faint); padding: 3px 8px; background: var(--bg-elev-2); border-radius: 6px; font-weight: 600; }
  @media (max-width: 768px) { .feature-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }
</style>
```

- [ ] **Step 1: Write `site/src/components/landing/CompareTable.astro`**

```astro
---
const yes = `<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"/></svg>`;
const no = `<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"><path d="M18 6L6 18M6 6l12 12"/></svg>`;
const rows = [
  { name: "Auto VM provision",                       a: yes, b: yes, c: yes },
  { name: "Multiple tunnels per VM",                 a: no,  b: yes, c: yes },
  { name: "Bin-packing scheduler",                   a: no,  b: no,  c: yes },
  { name: "Cheapest-fit instance type",              a: no,  b: no,  c: yes },
  { name: "Pool budget caps",                        a: no,  b: no,  c: yes },
  { name: "Drift detection + auto consolidation",    a: no,  b: no,  c: yes },
];
---
<section class="why">
  <div class="wrap">
    <div class="section-label">Compare</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Pick the right operator.</h2>
    <p class="section-sub">Same neighborhood, different priorities. Here's how the three stack up.</p>
    <div class="compare-table">
      <div class="compare-row compare-head">
        <span></span>
        <span class="col-name">inlets-operator</span>
        <span class="col-name">chisel-operator</span>
        <span class="col-name col-frp">frp-operator</span>
      </div>
      {rows.map((r) => (
        <div class="compare-row">
          <span class="feature-name">{r.name}</span>
          <span class={`cell ${r.a === yes ? 'cell-yes' : 'cell-no'}`} set:html={r.a}></span>
          <span class={`cell ${r.b === yes ? 'cell-yes' : 'cell-no'}`} set:html={r.b}></span>
          <span class={`cell ${r.c === yes ? 'cell-yes' : 'cell-no'}`} set:html={r.c}></span>
        </div>
      ))}
      <div class="compare-row">
        <span class="feature-name">Tunnel protocol</span>
        <span class="cell cell-partial">inlets</span>
        <span class="cell cell-partial">chisel</span>
        <span class="cell cell-partial cell-frp-text">frp (TCP/UDP/HTTP/HTTPS)</span>
      </div>
      <div class="compare-foot">Last verified against inlets-operator v0.18 and chisel-operator main · 2026-05-06.</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<style>
  .why { padding: 96px 0; background: var(--bg-elev-2); margin-top: 64px; border-top: 1px solid var(--line); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); }
  .compare-table { background: var(--bg-elev-1); border-radius: var(--r-lg); overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--line); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); }
  .compare-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.6fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; align-items: center; padding: 14px 22px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); }
  .compare-row:last-child { border-bottom: 0; }
  .compare-head { background: var(--bg-elev-2); padding: 18px 22px; }
  .compare-head .col-name { font-family: var(--font-heading); font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; }
  .compare-head .col-name.col-frp { color: var(--accent); position: relative; }
  .compare-head .col-name.col-frp::after { content: "ours"; display: inline-block; margin-left: 6px; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; padding: 2px 6px; background: var(--accent); color: var(--on-accent); border-radius: 999px; vertical-align: middle; }
  .feature-name { color: var(--ink-base); font-weight: 500; font-size: 15px; }
  .cell { text-align: center; }
  .cell :global(svg) { width: 22px; height: 22px; }
  .cell-yes { color: var(--accent); }
  .cell-no  { color: #94a3b8; }
  .cell-partial { color: var(--ink-muted); font-size: 13px; }
  .cell-frp-text { color: var(--accent); font-weight: 600; }
  .compare-foot { padding: 18px 22px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--ink-faint); background: var(--bg-elev-2); border-top: 1px solid var(--line); }
  @media (max-width: 768px) { .compare-row { grid-template-columns: 1.4fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 12px 14px; font-size: 13px; } }
</style>
```

---
---
<section class="install" id="install">
  <div class="wrap">

    <h2 class="section-title">Install via Helm.</h2>
    <p class="section-sub">Two charts: CRDs first, then the operator. Pulls from GHCR's OCI registry.</p>

      <span class="string">oci://ghcr.io/mtaku3/charts/frp-operator-crd</span> \
      <span class="flag">--version</span> <span class="string">0.0.1</span> \
      <span class="flag">--namespace</span> <span class="string">frp-operator-system</span> <span class="flag">--create-namespace</span>

<span class="prompt">$</span> <span class="cmd">helm upgrade --install frp-operator \</span>
      <span class="string">oci://ghcr.io/mtaku3/charts/frp-operator</span> \
      <span class="flag">--version</span> <span class="string">0.0.1</span> \
      <span class="flag">--namespace</span> <span class="string">frp-operator-system</span>

<span class="comment"># Operator running. Apply your first ExitPool next.</span></code></pre></div>
  </div>
</section>

<style>
  .install { padding: 96px 0; }
  .install-card { background: var(--code-bg); color: var(--code-ink); border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 28px 32px; max-width: 720px; margin: 0 auto; box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.7; overflow-x: auto; }
  .install-card pre { margin: 0; }
  .install-card code { font-family: inherit; background: transparent; color: inherit; padding: 0; }
  .prompt  { color: var(--code-stone); }
  .cmd     { color: var(--code-ink); }
  .flag    { color: var(--code-amber); }
  .string  { color: var(--code-lime); }
  .comment { color: var(--code-stone); font-style: italic; }
</style>

## Phase 5 — CI + deploy

### Task 18: CI drift gate

**Files:** Modify: `.github/workflows/lint.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Append `site-gen-drift` job to `lint.yml`**

Add as a sibling of the existing `helm-lint` job:

```yaml
  site-gen-drift:
    name: Site reference drift gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod
      - name: Install rsync
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y rsync
      - run: make site-gen
      - run: git diff --exit-code site/src/content/docs/reference/
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify YAML**

```bash
yq '.' .github/workflows/lint.yml > /dev/null
```

Expected: exit 0. (If `yq` isn't installed locally, skip — CI parses it.)

```bash

git commit -m "ci(site): drift gate for generated reference docs"
```

### Task 19: README site link + Vercel setup note

**Files:** Modify: `README.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Append a `## Docs site` section**

Insert after the `## Install` section (just above `## Getting Started`):

```markdown
## Docs site

Full documentation lives at [frp-operator.vercel.app](https://frp-operator.vercel.app).

The site is an Astro Starlight project under `site/`. Reference pages
(CRDs, Helm values, operator flags) are generated from code via
`make site-gen` and committed to git so Vercel builds without a Go
toolchain. Deploy is handled by Vercel's GitHub integration on push to
`main`; PRs get unique preview URLs automatically.

Local dev:

```sh
make site-gen
cd site && pnpm install && pnpm dev   # http://localhost:4321
```

```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add README.md
git commit -m "docs(site): link docs site + local dev instructions"
```

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat(site): landing page + Astro Starlight docs site" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- New \`site/\` directory: Astro 5 + Starlight + Tailwind. Single combined site — landing on \`/\`, docs on \`/docs/*\`.
- Diátaxis-shaped docs IA: getting-started (4 stubs), guides (10 stubs), concepts (5 stubs), reference (auto-generated CRDs / Helm values / flags + hand-written metrics + ADRs + karpenter conformance).
- Auto-generation: \`make site-gen\` runs \`crd-ref-docs\`, \`helm-docs\`, and a new \`hack/gen-flags-doc.go\` (Go AST → MDX). Generated MDX committed to git so Vercel builds without Go toolchain.
- Theme tokens (cream + amber-600, Fredoka + Nunito) live in \`site/src/styles/tokens.css\`; consumed by both landing components and Starlight via \`customCss\`.
- CI drift gate in \`lint.yml\` runs \`make site-gen && git diff --exit-code site/src/content/docs/reference/\`.

## Vercel setup (one-time, manual)
- Connect repo to Vercel.
- Project → Root Directory: \`site\`.
- Framework preset auto-detects Astro.
- Production branch: \`main\`. PR previews on by default.

## Test plan
- [x] \`pnpm build\` clean for site
- [x] \`make site-gen && git diff --exit-code site/src/content/docs/reference/\` clean
- [x] All Diátaxis sidebar groups populate (4 + 10 + 5 + 6 entries)
- [x] Landing renders Hero, ValueStrip, Features, CompareTable, InstallSnippet, Footer
- [ ] CI drift gate passes
- [ ] Vercel preview deploys cleanly from PR

## Spec / plan
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-landing-docs-design.md
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-06-landing-docs-implementation.md
EOF
)"

## Self-review (controller pre-flight)

**Spec coverage:**
- §"Repo layout" → Tasks 2, 3, 14 (site dir + components).
- §"IA — Landing" → Tasks 14, 15, 16, 17.
- §"IA — Docs" → Tasks 10, 11, 12, 13.
- §"Visual tokens" → Task 4.
- §"Starlight overrides" → Task 4.
- §"Tailwind binding" → Task 3.
- §"Auto-generation pipeline" → Tasks 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
- §"Hosting + deploy" → Task 19 (Vercel setup is one-time manual; documented in README + PR body).
- §"CI drift gate" → Task 18.
- §"Migration / repo touchpoints" → all touched, verified by checklist above.

**Type / name consistency:**
- `frp-operator.vercel.app` referenced consistently in `astro.config.mjs` (Task 3), `Landing.astro` og-url (Task 14), README (Task 19), spec.
- `--bg-base / --accent / --ink-base` etc. token names identical across `tokens.css` (Task 4), `tailwind.config.mjs` (Task 3), and component styles.
- `LoadConfigFromArgs` / `applyEnv` function names in `gen-flags-doc.go` (Task 8) match real names in `pkg/operator/flags.go`.
- Generated reference paths consistent: `site/src/content/docs/reference/{crds/,adrs/,flags.mdx,helm-values.mdx,karpenter-conformance.mdx,metrics.mdx,index.mdx}` referenced same in Tasks 9, 13, 18.
- Vercel root directory `site/` consistent across spec + Task 19 README.

**Placeholders:** none. Every step has full code, full commands, and `Expected:` verifications. Stub MDX content is intentional and explicitly flagged as `:::caution` admonitions (not "TODO" placeholders) — those are committed-as-stubs that the user will fill post-launch.

**Known dev/CI assumptions:**
- Repo Makefile already has the kubebuilder `go-install-tool` macro and `LOCALBIN` variable. If not, Task 5 needs to copy them from a kubebuilder reference.
- `crd-ref-docs` v0.1.0 and `helm-docs` v1.14.2 are the latest at writing time. CI fetches both at job start; first run is slow (~30s for `go install`). Subsequent runs use Go cache.
- Vercel project creation is a one-time manual step that the user does in the dashboard. The PR cannot enable auto-deploy on its own.
